The Municipal Accountant will further examine the “Summary of Expenditures recorded as Expenses” as per AOM No. 2021-07 (2020), to evaluate and assess whether those expenses will be considered as “Assets”. observations.reason: Based on the Obligation Request attached to each Disbursement Voucher the charging of this accounts were made to expenses and not charged to Capital Outlay (Asset). Moreover, the program of work for the establishment of isolation unit was prepared on a piecemeal basis, thus, precluding the Municipal Accountant to evaluate whether or not such materials and labor will be considered as “Asset”.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through its GAD Focal Person:

Implement the PPAs in the GPB in order to fully provide the constituents the benefits that could be derived from the identified gender-responsive PPAs.




Review the mathematical accuracy of the budget allocation in the GPB and issue the prescribed forms as annexed by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA-JMC No. 2016-01.




Submit the GPB to the COA Audit Team within five working days from receipt of the approved plans as well as the GAD AR within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year. | See to it that identified gender-responsive PPAs will be implemented by the LGU for the benefit of the constituents.




The present GAD Focal Person will see to it that the prescribed form as annexed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA-JMC No. 2016-01 will be used.
Endorsed to our GAD Focal the said date of submission of GAD AR every 15th of January.
observations.reason:
Due to the Impending surge of cases in the municipality and of the implementation of various community quarantine category, the GPB was not fully implemented because forum and gatherings were limited to ensure the safety and health of everyone.




No submission of GAD AR were made by the GAD Focal as of the report date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) issue a  directive
      to the Property Officer, the Municipal Accountant  and  the  Inventory
      Committee to determine the composition of the unaccounted/missing PPEs
      totaling P31 Million, verify  if  not  yet  disposed  or  transferred,
      identify the person/s accountable thereof, and include the same in the
      Report on the Physical Count of PPE for CY 2022.

  We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant (MA)  review  the
      variances between the book balance  and  RPCPPE  and  start  with  the
      process on the one-time cleansing of PPE  accounts  by  following  the
      procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

        a)  Review  current  and  previous   transactions   affecting   the
           Construction in Progress accounts to determine  possible  errors
           made and to confirm the composition of the accounts;



        b) Reconcile with the records of the Municipal Engineering  Office,
           particularly on-going projects which were previously  classified
           as Construction in Progress and prepare the necessary correcting
           entries, where necessary;



        c) Maintain an  updated  Subsidiary  Ledger  for  each  project  to
           support the GL balance of CIP accounts; and



        d) Disclose the details  of  CIP  accounts  in  the  Notes  to  the
           Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include in the  Notes  to
      the Financial Statements  the  lacking  relevant  information  on  PPE
      accounts as required by IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer provide the Municipal
      Accountant with the complete documents and  reports  relative  to  the
      vaccines received from  the  DOH,  as  well  as  its  distribution  or
      issuance, so that the vaccines will be properly recorded, reported and
      completely accounted for.


   We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the  vaccines
      received by the LGU, based on the documents submitted by the  MHO,  as
      part of its inventory using the perpetual inventory system, and assist
      the Municipal Health Officer in the proper accounting of the  vaccines
      received, as well as its distribution and issuance.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer


        a) transfer the amount of ₱261,467.30 to  the  Special  Trust  Fund
           representing the balance of unexpended  QRF  and  MOOE  not  yet
           transferred;


        b) transfer, in the succeeding years, the unexpended balance of the
           QRF and MOOE BEFORE THE END OF EACH YEAR to avoid the  reversion
           of the unexpended LDRRMF to unappropriated surplus  and  ensure
           that said funds are ready for use in the  timely  implementation
           of the programmed PPAs.


   We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant
      and Municipal Budget Officer retain the unexpended Capital  Outlay  in
      the General Fund as Continuing Appropriations until the completion  of
      its intended purpose.

  We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate
      subsidiary ledgers for transfers of the LGU’s unutilized DRRMF to  the
      Trust Fund by year of transfer, for DRRMF from other LGUs by  LGU  and
      for other sources by donor recorded.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Budget  Officer,  the   Municipal
      Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:

        a) Review prior years’ transfer of cash to the 20% Development Fund
           DBP bank account to identify the causes of  cash  insufficiency;
           and transfer immediately to  the  said  bank  account  the  cash
           equivalent for the 20% DF Current and Continuing  Appropriations
           to ensure that the DF payables and the balance of appropriations
           as of December 31, 2021 are fully covered with the corresponding
           cash allocation; and


        b) Transfer to the  DF  DBP  bank  account  the  full  20%  of  the
           succeeding IRA as soon as it is downloaded to the LGU.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Health  Officer  and  Municipal
      Accountant submit the following documents to validate the  eligibility
      and entitlement of the claimants  and  support  the  validity  of  the
      payments.

        a)  Duly  accomplished  DTRs,  or  equivalent  documents,  of   all
           recipients of COVID-19 AHDP and SRA, as  evidence  of  the  days
           that the HWs and HRHs physically  reported  for  work  at  their
           assigned work stations; and

        b) Certification of the Municipal Health Officer that the claimants
           were  assigned  at  the  Rural  Health  Unit  or  in  the  LGU’s
           quarantine facility.

We recommended that the GSO – Designate submit to the Audit  Team  the
      list of beneficiaries who received the welfare/food  packs  or  relief
      goods, showing the names, addresses and signatures of the  recipients,
      as well as the quantities received,  to  prove  that  the  items  were
      actually received by the intended beneficiaries.

  We further recommended  that,  henceforth,  the  Municipal  Accountant
      record  the  distribution  of  welfare  goods  only  when   adequately
      supported with proof of actual receipt by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  stop  the  practice  of
      leaving  pre-signed  checks  during  her  official  travels  or  other
      instances of her absence from the municipality.

   We further recommended  that,  henceforth,  to  ensure  continuity  of
      normal operations even when the LCE is on official travel or otherwise
      absent, adhere to the provisions of Section 345 of RA 7160, the  Local
      Government  Code  of  the  Philippines  (LGCP),  which  mandates   the
      succession of responsibility over the signing of checks.

We recommended that the Bookkeeper and the Municipal Accountant:

Record in the appropriate expense of asset accounts any amount utilized or paid from the funds received from NGAs; and

Record a reduction (debit) in the Due to NGAs account and the corresponding revenue account only when the purpose of the project and program is completed and the Fund Utilization Reports/Liquidation Report is submitted to the source agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the regulations provided for in the Local Treasury Operations Manual by performing the following:

Require the Collecting Officers (COs) to turn-over all collections supported with the Report of Collections and Deposits/Remittances prepared on a daily basis or as receipt of collections/remittances arise, and regularly check cashbook of the COs;

Deposit all received collections intact and daily or not later than the next banking day; and

Refrain from issuing additional accountable forms when collections of existing issued accountable forms are not yet turned-over by the Collecting Officer concerned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider making a study on the overall organizational structure of the LGU with emphasis on the staffing patterns to correct the wide salary gaps among employees for optimal delivery of public service.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer be required to submit at the beginning of the year a list of on-going and to be implemented projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for the current year, and inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period by preparing and maintaining complete and updated subsidiary records and see to it that no additional cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones are settled or properly accounted for in the books;




Refrain from approving claims of cash advance without the certification from the Accountant attesting to the effect that the accountable officer has no unliquidated cash advance.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accounting Office monitors and is strictly enforcing the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period. Subsidiary records are maintained and checked regularly. Moreover, no additional cash advances were allowed unless previous cash advances are properly accounted for in the books.
Cash advances are not granted without a certification from the Municipal Accountant, that the employee has no unliquidated cash advance. Certification is written by the Municipal Accountant on the face of the DV before approving the disbursement voucher.

As a starting point, we also suggest that  items acquired in the year 2012 be recorded promptly in the PPELC to assure that data is available for the next accounting period.

1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to
analyze the reciprocal accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies
and adjust the books accordingly; henceforth, to conduct a periodic
reconciliation of reciprocal accounts.

1.3. We recommended that management file a request for authority to
write-off dormant accounts totaling P690,021.10 to the COA Audit Team
pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

3.9 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with
the regulations provided for in the Local Treasury Operations Manual by
performing the following:
a) Require the Collecting Officers (COs) to turn-over all collections
supported with the Report of Collections and
Deposits/Remittances prepared on a daily basis or as receipt of
collection/remittances arises, and regularly check cashbook of the
COs;
b) Deposit all received collections intact and daily or not later than
the next banking day; and
c) Refrain from issuing additional accountable forms when
collections of existing issued accountable forms are not yet
turned-over by the Collecting Officer concerned

4.7 We recommended that management limit the appropriation and
utilization of the 20% DF for purposes that are outlined in RA 7160 and DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

5.6 We recommended that management strictly adhere to Section 325(h)
of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, in the
appropriation and obligation of extraordinary and miscellaneous
expenses.

6.17 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider making a
study on the overall organizational structure of the LGU with emphasis on the
staffing patterns to correct the wide salary gaps among employees for optimal
delivery of public service.

6.18 Likewise, fill up the vacant positions by offering it first to qualified
employees subject to the minimum standards and guidelines prescribed by the
Civil Service Commission (CSC).

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer allow only payroll
deductions to loan amortization by considering that net take home pay is within the
mandated amount of P 5,000.00.

1.8 We recommended that the Inventory Committee be required to complete the
reconciliation of the physical count of PPE with the records of the General Services
Office and the Accounting Office to arrive at the correct balances of PPE accounts in
the financial statements. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to:
(i) Record the donated PPE based on declared value in the deed of donation
or the bill of lading and other related documents. In the absence of
declared value, use the fair value or appraised value of the PPE;
(ii) Draw a journal entry voucher to derecognize the properties with no future
economic benefits or service potential expected from its use or disposal;
and
(iii) Prepare and submit a detailed lapsing schedule in order to ascertain the
accuracy of the reported deprecation on PPE.

2.7 We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local
Roads Asset Management System outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015 be strictly adhered to, by requiring the following officials to:
(i) The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to
reconcile their records and adjust their records accordingly;
(ii) The Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule for the
computation of depreciation and disclose the road network system in
the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
(iii) The General Services Officer to maintain Local Road Inventory and
Road Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, as
well as prepare a Report on Local Road Network.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to transfer the
unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of CY 2014-2018 to the Special Trust Fund in
the Trust Fund Books, and require the MDRRMC to present the list of projects and
activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years under a separate
caption in the LDRRMFIP. Likewise, require the MDRRMO and Municipal
Accountant to submit a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF
within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

4.7 We recommended that the LDRRM Council be directed to include in its
LDRRMFIP, only programs/projects/activities that are related to disaster
management activities in order to ensure that the LDRRMF is appropriated for valid
programs/projects/ activities only, pursuant to Section 21 of RA 10121, Items A & B
of DILG MC No. 2012-73 and Item 5 of the NDRRMC/DBM/DILG JMC No. 2013-
01. Likewise, stop the practice of charging unrelated expenses to the LDRRMF.

5.9 We recommended that the GAD Focal Person strictly comply with Section 36
of RA 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01,
to ensure that the programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD related
undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection,
and other assistance that may be provided. Likewise, strictly adhere to Item V of
COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for timely submission of the said
reports to the COA Audit Team.

6.8 We recommended that henceforth, Contract of Service of job order personnel
shall state the specific duties and responsibilities, as well as the place/area of
assignment of the personnel and the payment of their wages shall be supported with
quantified accomplishment reports, as provided for Section 4 of PD 1445, CSC
Memorandum Circular No. 17, s. 2002, promulgating CSC Resolution No. 02-0790
dated June 5, 2002, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Likewise,
involve the human resource department in the selection and hiring of job-order
personnel.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer’s Office be required to
regularly monitor contractor’s compliance with the posting of signboards complete
with the information required in COA Circular No. 2013-04 dated January 30, 2013.
We recommend that the Accounting Unit maintain complete SLs.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Bookkeeper- Designate prepares almost all the financial reports hence, she could no longer prepare the SLs.
observations.reason: Lack of competent personnel who could prepare the complete SLs. The Bookkeeper-Designate could no longer prepare the SLs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: 1. The Local Chief Executive immediately hire personnel such as Municipal Accountant and Accounting Staffs who are competent and enough with the duties and responsibilities in the Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: AAR Verification of the ChkDJ and JEVs revealed that there are still transactions when journalized have no full pledge debit/credit entry. The Bookkeeper-Designate explained that at times she finds it difficult determine the appropriate account to either debit/credit.
observations.reason: There are still personnel hired such as Municipal Accountant and Accounting Staffs when journalized still have no full pledge debit/credit entry. The Bookkeeper-Designate sometimes finds it difficult determine the appropriate account to either debit/credit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer: a. Review their respective records (GL and Cashbook) to account for the difference of P53,732.93 by comparing each other’s entries in their respective records; b. Effect the recommended adjustments in their respective records to come up with the accurate Cash-Local Treasury account balance; c. Henceforth, conduct thorough periodic reconciliation between their respective records since various errors in recording and reconciling items still exist.
observations.reason: There are still variances and various errors noted in the books of accounts and cash of the MT due to improper recording and timing in both of their books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: direct the Municipal Chief Executive to refrain from charging procurement of goods/services to special cash advances and ensure that procurement of goods/services shall be competitive and transparent and that procurement shall go through public bidding and/or other modes of procurement authorized under RA 9184.
observations.reason: Management did not give any reason for the non-compliance of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the executive officers strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular 2011-059 and COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid the unnecessary incurrence of expenditures for unnecessary and excessive registration fees and expenditures.
observations.reason: Management did not give any reason for the non-compliance of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Unit ensure that official receipts are attached and signed by Payee on the DV.
observations.reason: Box D of DVs are not always signed. Random verification of disbursements in March, July and August of 2017 disclosed that signature was lacking in Box D of a number of DVs. Moreover, evidence of receipt of payment was not always attached, e.g. AICS no AR and REALBO Registration no OR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to demand from the Accountable Collecting Officers to remit daily the full amount collected promptly and intact; b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to check first the inventory of accountable forms to justify the issuance of additional booklets. See to it that all issued accountable forms be submitted and likewise, collections thereof be remitted.
observations.reason: Field collectors could not immediately remit their collections because preparation of the report of collections and reconciliation with the actual collections on hand requires time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to strictly deposit all collections promptly and intact to ensure that government funds are properly accounted for and safeguarded against loss or misuse.
observations.reason: The Cash -Local Treasury Account has a monthly average balance of P 330,081.40 which shows that collections are not deposited promptly and intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Accountable Officers to issue accountable forms strictly in numerical sequence.
observations.reason: Post-audit revealed that accountable forms were still not strictly issued in numerical sequence. The Municipal Treasurer did not comment. He committed to comply in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Conduct the physical inventory and submit the inventory report; b. Require the Accounting and General Services/Property Services/Property Unit to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers, Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively and reconcile the balances of the accounts in the general ledger against the individual subsidiary/property ledger cards including the inventory report prepared by the Property Unit.
observations.reason: Inventory report was requested but as of February 23, 2018, no inventory report was submitted to the Audit Team informing that the physical inventory of the agency’s PPE was conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant position be filled up soonest consistent with the provision of law.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant position is still unfilled as of February 23, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1. Require the officials concerned to immediately utilize the fund for the intended projects or programs in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations; 2. Prepare liquidation report of those projects completed which were funded out of the fund under the account Due to other NGAs and Due to LGUs and return to the source agency the balances not utilized.
observations.reason: Management commented that hopefully in the ensuing year fund transfers will be utilized and liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Enforce compliance with competitive bidding in the procurement of goods and services pursuant to Section 10 of RA 9184 and alternative methods should be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided for in the IRR; b. Pay purchases and regular expenses through the imprest system whereby payments by checks are made directly to the creditors or suppliers, or by the disbursing officer in the form of cash through his cash advance in the case of special time-bound undertaking; and c. Discontinue the practice of paying regular expenses on reimbursement basis.
observations.reason: Various reimbursements were still made during the year. Management did not discontinue the practice of reimbursement basis for payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: require the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer to conduct physical count of the Inventories, maintain stock cards and prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) as basis of the Accountant in the recording of the Inventory and reconcile the Inventory balance.
observations.reason: During the year inventories were directly recorded as expense account and no adjusting entry was made to recognize the asset portion of inventories. The Accounting Unit lacks staff to handle and maintain the supplies and inventory records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to officials and employees with outstanding cash advances and ensure that previous cash advances have been liquidated before granting new ones.
observations.reason: In the grant of cash advances, there were no Certifications issued by the officials and employees with previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to: (1) Set up and maintain a special account in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund; (2) Strictly adhere to section 105 and 110 of the NGAS Manual Volume 1 for LGUs and prepare the post- closing Trial Balance for each special account. Year-end financial statements, such as the Balance Sheet, Statement of Income and Expenses and Statement of Cash Flows, should be likewise prepared. These reports should form part of the schedules of the General Fund Trial Balance.
observations.managementAction: AAR No action yet from Management.
observations.reason: Management did not maintain separate special account and year-end reports for the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to exercise extra care in the processing of refunds to employees. The amounts claimed should be thoroughly verified as valid and available, and proof thereof should be indicated on the face of the DV as the Certification by the Accountant that the amount has not been remitted yet, therefore available for refund, and that the amount has been deleted from the remittance list to GOCCs for the period. Require the immediate deduction from the payroll for the amounts refunded to the employees which were in fact already remitted to the GOCCs.
observations.managementAction: AAR No action yet from Management.
observations.reason: Determination of the breakdown of that abnormal balance could not be undertaken due to lack of personnel in the Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to establish and determine the validity of unidentified creditors and dormant accounts and reconcile the Subsidiary Ledger and General Ledger and determine the cause for the negative balances, particularly the private lending institutions.
observations.managementAction: AAR No action yet from Management.
observations.reason: Determination of the validity and reconciliation of unidentified creditors and dormant accounts could not be undertaken due to lack of personnel in the Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials concerned to stop the practice of procuring petroleum products on reimbursement basis; enforce compliance with the Revised IRR of RA 9184; discontinue the practice of procuring goods and services on reimbursement basis and strictly adhere to payment made by check pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated Feb. 10, 1997.
observations.reason: Management could not give any reason(s) for the non-compliance of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to strictly
observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA
7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and
Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommend for the immediate liquidation of fund transfers received from
the Provincial Government of Leyte, and to obtain from the Province of Leyte the
covering MOA to ensure compliance on the utilization and liquidation of fund
transfers. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to submit a report on the
utilization of fund transfers within ten (10) days after end of each month/end of the
agreed period for the Project pursuant to Sections 4.6 and 4.7 of COA Circular No.
94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer be required to submit at the
beginning of the year a list of on-going and to be implemented
projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for the current year, and inform COA within
ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the
program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are
already posted as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that the BAC and BAC Secretariat be required to strictly
observe all the procurement processes in the conduct of competitive bidding as
outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 and
submit the lacking procurement documents.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the results of post-audit once the
documents are submitted. Management committed to institute procedures to comply with
the audit recommendation.

We recommend that management procure common-use supplies and
equipment only at the DBM-PS Depot pursuant to Administrative Order No. 17
dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011. Common-
use supplies not available at the PS Depot should be procured in accordance with
the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, strictly observe the procedures in the conduct
of public bidding as outlined in the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that management stop the practice of paying regular
expenses on reimbursement basis or thru petty cash fund, instead payment should
be made directly to the supplier or creditor thru check as required in Section 2 of
COA Circular No. 97-002. Henceforth, adequately plan its procurement for fuel, oil
and lubricants and follow strictly the procedures laid down in the Revised IRR of
RA 9184. Likewise, management should prepare and submit the required reports
and properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets as prescribed in COA Circular No.
77-61 dated September 26, 1977. Moreover, approval of all travel tickets should not
be delegated to an official lower in rank than an Assistant Chief.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all employees and
officials to submit electronic copy of official receipts instead of RERs to support
claims of taxi fare. Likewise, we recommend the use of the certification for expenses
not requiring receipts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2017-001.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance
where necessary

We recommend that management consider filling in the vacant positions for
more effective administration of those offices and to deliver the basic services needed
by the constituents. Likewise, strictly observe Section 443 and 474 of Republic Act
No. 7160, the Civil Service Rules on Designation and Section 27 of the Administrative
Code of the Philippines in the hiring of applicants to the vacant positions. Moreover,
see to it that only qualified and competent personnel should be appointed to perform
duties and functions of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Bookkeeper in
order to generate accurate and timely accounting reports.

We recommend that management increase the fidelity bond of the Municipal
Treasurer to an amount sufficient to cover the maximum accountability on cash he
handles for a given period, in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium
Rates provided by the Bureau of the Treasury, so as to protect the interest of the
LGU in case of defalcation, shortages and unrelieved losses in the account of the
Municipal Treasurer.
We recommend that management submit the 2016-1 already submit the accounts on time.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Financial Statements were submitted. See related discussions on Finding No. 1 of this report.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel unable the LGU to submit the accounts on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly adhere to Section 475 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations Registries were not submitted for CY 2016 to CY 2017.
observations.reason: Although the Municipal Budget Officer submitted Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (SAAOB), corresponding Registries were not submitted for CY 2016 to CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1. Municipal Treasurer refrain from drawing checks to recorded in the Disbursement Vouchers not completely supported with the necessary documentary requirements. 2. Municipal Accountant ensure proper recording of transactions in the period of its occurrence consistent with the objectivity principle and PPSAS No. 1 on accrual basis of accounting.
observations.reason: Some DVs still lacks the needed attachments and signatures of concerned personnel, thus recorded in the preceding months.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the: Petty Cash Custodians strictly adhere to Section 48, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012- 001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Some recurring and non-petty expenses were not supported with the necessary documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the: 1. Immediately liquidate the cash advance for petty cash fund; and 2. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 339 of the Local Government Code (RA 7160), Section 75 of PD 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and stop the practice of drawing cash advances from the coffers of the LGU.
observations.reason: Although the additional petty cash advance was granted to the LCE, the LCE liquidated his cash advance last Nov. 2016 and designated a Social Welfare Officer as the new Accountable Officer to handle the PCF for AICS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period by preparing and maintaining complete and updated subsidiary records and see to it that no additional cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones are settled or properly accounted for in the books; 2. Refrain from approving claims of cash advance without the certification from the Accountant attesting to the effect that the accountable officer has no unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: In some instances various special cash advances were granted to the Municipal Treasurer despite existence of unliquidated cash advances. The Municipal Treasurer explained that among the AOs of the LGU, his bond could handle the largest accountability that is why regular and special cash advances are granted to him.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the: 1. Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer refrain from drawing checks in the absence of certification from the Municipal Accountant; and 2. Strictly adhere to paragraph 4 and 5 of Section 4 of the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines (PD 1445), pertinent provisions of NGAS Manual for LGUs and rules on granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that: require the accountable officers to be duly authorized/designated as Disbursing Officer properly and secured the required fidelity bond pursuant to Section 305(f) of RA 7160, Section 4.1.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002, and Treasury Circular No. 2-2009.
observations.reason: Some special cash advances were granted to municipal employees without being authorized/designated as Disbursing Officer properly and without bond requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: 1. The Local Chief Executive establish a policy that all purchases of supplies and direct inventory are centrally done and tasked the General Services Office who is tasked to handle the supplies from procurements to issuances to end users; 2. Accounting Unit maintain the Supplies and Inventory records and Stock cards using the Delivery Receipt and Inspected by the Inspectorate Committee; 3. Custodian of each department prepare a quarterly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) supported with the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) to be consolidated in the GSO and submitted to the Accounting Unit as basis for the periodic recognition of inventory expense; 4. Property Custodian of each department conduct a physical count of supplies on hand; prepare and submit to the Accounting Unit through the GSO, an inventory list of supplies and materials stock on hand at year-end so the inventory account balances presented in the Financial Statements reflects the actual unused supplies at year-end; 5. Accounting Unit exhaust all possible means to produce the necessary inventory reports and records to support the non-moving accounts and file the appropriate request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for its dropping from the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Some inventories for the year were directly recorded as outright expense account and no adjusting entry was recorded to recognize the asset portion of inventories. The Accounting Unit explained that they lack personnel who could handle the monitoring and recordkeeping of the Supplies and Inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a) Submit its Annual Procurement Plan for CY 2016; b) Strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 09-2009 and the IRR of R.A. 9184.
observations.reason: During the year, no Annual Procurement Plan was submitted by the LGU. Review of the disbursement vouchers showed that in most of the trainings, meetings and seminars in the procurement of goods if one and same adopting supplier caters the food services of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1. Require the Accounting Unit to always adopt payroll system in payment of wages to its laborers for construction projects instead of lump sum payment to a lone laborer because doing so is tantamount to irregular expenditures as provided in item 4 of COA Circular No. 77-5 dated March 29, 1977. 2. Ensure that claims of wages for laborers shall be completely supported such as Contract of Services, Individual Program of Work and Detailed Estimate, Straight line Diagram or Plans/Drawings and Community Tax Certificate of the Laborer and Certificate of Availability of Funds, if charge against the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Management did not fully implement payroll system for payment of wages to laborers and some claims lacked complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to stop the inclusion of programs and projects which are not among the enumerated in Section 3.0 to 4.0 of the DILG-DBM Joint Circular. Instead of disbursing the 20% component of the Internal Revenue Allotment for short-term programs and projects that would benefit only a few constituents of the municipality, the developmental fund must be directed to the attainment of desired social, economic development and environmental outcomes that would benefit the municipality in the long-run.
observations.reason: Management included programs and projects not among the enumerated in the Joint Circular. Management explained that the improper charges to the 20% fund still exist because they considered the programs and projects as developmental.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop using the 20% component of the Internal Revenue Allotment which is intended for developmental projects for entertainment like holding of Mr. & Miss Face Body Shot and Mr. & Miss Tolosa 2016, giving out prizes for games, talent and sports competitions, fireworks display, rental of equipment and other various related expenses in the celebration of the Tolosa Day and Summer Sports Festival since doing so is contrary to Section 343 of the RA 7160 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, the aforementioned expenditures were taken from Transfers from the Provincial Government of Leyte.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require the recipient individual or group to submit (1) a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement embodying the terms and conditions to be followed in the utilization of the assistance; (2) inspection report and certificate of project completion rendered/issued by the GO authorized representative upon completion of the project for the purpose of liquidation; and (3) list of beneficiaries with their acceptance/acknowledgement of the project/fund/goods/services received. We also recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant-Designate refrain from processing payments/releases of financial assistance if such are not supported with the aforesaid supporting documents in order that all financial assistance accrue to the benefit of the constituents.

We recommend that each accountable officer ensure the renewal of their fidelity bond before its expiration date in compliance with Section 101 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to stamp “PAID” all vouchers, payrolls and their supporting documents after the corresponding check is signed and issued to the intended payees in strict compliance with Section 2 (Q) of COA Circular No. 92-389 dated November 3, 1992.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer: 1. Refrain from drawing checks in the absence of certification from the Municipal Accountant; and 2. Strictly adhere to paragraph 4 and 5 of Section 4 of the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines (PD 1445), pertinent provisions of NGAS Manual for LGUs and rules on internal control for disbursements.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and Department Heads fast track/ prioritize the implementation of prior years’ audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Management gave assurance the recommendations will be implemented in the current year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee to submit supporting procurement documents for all stages of the public bidding process as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-0001 dated February 12, 2009 and RA 9184 and its IRR as amended on September 2, 2009.
observations.reason: Due to other regular duties and functions some documents still lack supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.reason: Management could not give reason(s) for the non-compliance of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the documentation specific for every transaction pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 and instruct the Accountant to ensure completeness of documentation before affixing signature on the disbursement voucher.

We recommend strict compliance to Section 73 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 93-404. Otherwise, the accountable officer shall not be relieved from the accountability or be allowed credit for the loss during the settlement of his account.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to provide and compute the correct allowance for depreciation and to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 4-0 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1 and Section 58 of PD 1445. As a starting point, we also suggest that items acquired in the year 2012 be recorded promptly in the PPELC to assure that data is available for the next accounting period.
observations.managementAction: AAR Although depreciation of PPEs totaling P6,811,709.93 was provided to PPEs totaling P25,223,188.36, no lapsing schedule was submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Depreciation of properties totaling P6,811,709.93 was provided however no lapsing schedule was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Accountable Officers to issue accountable forms strictly in numerical sequence.
observations.reason: Due to various assignments of the BAC TWG tasked to submit the perfected bid-out contracts for legal and auditorial review these are not completely supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit supporting procurement documents for all stages of the public bidding process as prescribed in the COA Circular No. 2009-0001 dated February 12, 2009 and RA 9184 and its IRR as amended on September 2, 2009.
observations.reason: These documents are not completely supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned officials to adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.reason: These are not completely supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: Instruct the Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentation for every transaction charged pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the position of Municipal Accountant be filled up soonest consistent with the provision of law.
observations.reason: DVs and supporting journals, JEVs, ledgers, financial reports revealed: (1) several misclassified accounts and errors in recording; (2) incomplete entries (account title/account code) (debit/credit) in JEVs and Journals; (3) several undated and unnumbered DVs/PVs, POs, PRs and other accounting forms; (4) error in mathematical computation of running balances in GLs; (5) no subsidiary schedules to support control accounts; (6) presence of over/under withholding of tax; (7) refund of payments/contributions to GOCCs and other financial institutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1.Require the municipal concerned to immediately utilize the fund for the intended projects or programs in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations; 2.Prepare liquidation reports of those projects completed which were funded out of the fund under the account Due to other NGAs and Due to LGUs and return to the source agency the balances not utilized.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the balance of Due to NGAs and LGUs are P 29,487,715.39 and P 4,777,122.36 respectively, or a consolidated balance of P 34,264,837.75 which represents 95.42% of the total Inter-Agency Payable account. Management commented that hopefully in the ensuing year fund transfers will be utilized and eventually liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Enforce compliance with the competitive bidding in procurement of goods and services pursuant to Section 10 of RA 9184 and payment for purchases only in exceptional cases as provided for in the IRR; b. Pay purchases and regular expenses through the imprest system whereby payments by checks are made directly to the creditors or suppliers, or by the disbursing officer in the form of cash through his cash advance in the case of special time-bound undertaking; and c. Discontinue the practice of paying regular expenses on reimbursement basis.
observations.reason: Various reimbursements were still made such as the purchase of office supplies by different departments; purchase of gasoline; purchase of T-shirts and printing; snacks; sports equipment; repair of motor vehicle; purchase of spare parts; other supplies and materials in connection with Tolosa Day.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to adhere to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 95-006 and submit required documents timely; otherwise impose sanctions as provided.
observations.reason: Appropriate documents were not submitted within the time frame; management did not provide reasons. Recommend imposition of sanctions as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive strictly require the submission of request for relief of accountability and/or write-off and notify the auditor in writing as required under Section 73 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 93-404.
observations.reason: Except for the MTO who already furnished the team with the Notice of Loss, the rest of the departments committed to submit a copy to the current audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountant to send demand letters to officials and employees to remit their collections and to ensure liquidation; otherwise send demand letters and enforce liquidation.
observations.reason: The account Advances to Officers and Employees and Advances to Special Disbursing Officer have balances P 950,457.71 and P 1,993,900.95 respectively or P 2,944,358.66. In the grant of CAs, there are no Certificates from the Accountants that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountant to exercise extra care in the processing of refunds to employees; require the Certification on the face of the DV that the amount has not been remitted yet and that the amount has been deleted from the remittance list to GOCCs for the period; require immediate deduction from payroll for amounts refunded that were in fact already remitted to GOCCs.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel in the Accounting Unit, determination of the breakdown of said abnormal account could not be undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountant to establish and determine the validity of the unidentified creditors and dormant accounts and reconcile the Subsidiary Ledger and General Ledger balances and determine the cause for the negative balances, particularly the private lending institutions.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, Other Payables account have an abnormal balance of (P 118,118.75). Due to lack of personnel in the Accounting Unit, determination of the validity and cause for the negative balances could not be undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the monthly SAAOB for each fund and monthly pre-closing trial balances as prescribed; otherwise recommend the imposition of sanctions as Section 122 of PD 1445 on suspension of payment of salaries of concerned officials.
observations.reason: Only a Summarized SAAOB Report as of December 31, 2016 was submitted not supported with the required Registries last May 29, 2017. Demand letters were sent; monthly SAAOB was not submitted timely.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit the financial statements, reports
and transaction documents promptly and regularly for the succeeding years in
accordance with Section 41 (2) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Section
7.2.1 (a) of the Revised Rules on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).

We recommend that the Accounting Unit maintain complete subsidiary
ledgers for all accounts for the accounting system of the LGU.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly adhere to Section
475 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of PD 1445.

We recommend that:
1. The Local Chief Executive immediately hire personnel such as full pledge
Municipal Accountant and Accounting Staffs who are competent and
knowledgeable enough with the duties and responsibilities in the
Accounting Unit.
2. Meanwhile, the Municipal Accountant-Designate should carefully review
before approving the transactions recorded in the JEVs and Journals
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommend that the:
1. Municipal Treasurer refrain from drawing checks to Disbursement
Vouchers not completely supported with the necessary documentary
requirements.
2. Municipal Accountant ensure proper recording of transactions in the period
of its occurrence consistent with the objectivity principle and PPSAS No. 1
on accrual basis of accounting.

We recommend that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:
a. Review their respective records (GL and Cashbook) to account for the
difference of P53,732.93 by comparing each other’s entries in their
respective records;
b. Effect the recommended adjustments in their respective records to come up
with the accurate Cash-Local Treasury account balance;
c. Henceforth, conduct thorough periodic reconciliation between their
respective records since various errors in recording and reconciling items
still exist.

We recommend that the Petty Cash Custodians strictly adhere to Section 48,
Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and pertinent provisions of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that the LCE:
1. Immediately liquidate the cash advance for petty cash fund; and
2. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 339 of the Local Government
Code (RA 7160), pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and
Section 75 of PD 1445 and stop the practice of drawing cash advances
from the coffers of the LGU.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
1. require the Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the liquidation of
cash advances within the prescribed period by preparing and maintaining
complete and updated subsidiary records and see to it that no additional
cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones are settled or
properly accounted for in the books;
2. refrain from approving claims of cash advance without the certification
from the Accountant attesting to the effect that the accountable officer has
no unliquidated cash advances.

We recommend that the:
1. Local Chief Executive and Municipal Treasurer refrain from drawing
checks in the absence of signature/certification by the Municipal
Accountant-Designate on the DV;
2. Strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that management grant cash advances only to employees duly
authorized/designated as Disbursing Officer properly bonded pursuant to Section
305(f) of RA 7160, Section 4.1.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002, and Treasury Circular
No. 2-2009.

We recommend that the:
1. The Local Executive establish a policy that all purchases of supplies and
inventory are centrally done and made through the General Services
Office who is tasked to handle supplies from procurements to issuances to
end users;
2. Accounting Unit record the receipt and issuance of Supplies and Inventory
in the corresponding Supplies and Stock Cards using the Delivery Receipt
already marked Inspected by the Inspectorate Committee;
3. Custodian of each department prepare a quarterly Report of Supplies
and Materials Issued (RSMI) supported with the Requisition and Issue
Slip (RIS) to be consolidated in the General Services Office (GSO) and
submitted to the Accounting Unit as basis for the periodic recognition of
inventory expense;
4. Property Custodian of each department conduct a physical count of
supplies on hand; prepare and submit to the Accounting Unit through the
GSO, an inventory list of supplies and materials stock on hand at year-end
so the inventory account balances presented in the Financial Statements
reflects the actual unused supplies at year-end;
5. Accounting Unit exhaust all possible means to produce the necessary
inventory reports and records to support the non-moving accounts and file
the appropriate request for authority to write-off dormant accounts
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for its
dropping from the books of accounts.

We recommend that management:
a) Submit its Annual Procurement Plan for CY 2016.
b) Strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 09-
2009 and IRR of R.A. 9184 in the procurement of goods if adopting
shopping/small value procurement as one of the alternative modes of
procurement.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the declared policy of the
government for the promotion of good governance, the principles of transparency,
accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement process and ensure
that procurement of goods/services shall be competitive and transparent, and
therefore shall go through public bidding and/or other modes of procurement
authorized under RA 9184.

We recommend that management:
1. require the Accounting Unit to always adopt payroll system in the payment
of wages to its laborers for construction projects instead of lump sum
payment to a lone laborer because doing so is tantamount to expenditures
considered as irregular as provided in item 4 of COA Circular No. 77-5
dated March 29, 1977.
2. ensure that claims of wages for laborers shall be completely supported such
as Contract of Services, Individual Program of Work and Detailed
Estimate, Straight line Diagram or Plans/Drawings and Community Tax
Certificate of the Laborer and Certificate of Availability of Funds, if charge
against the Trust Fund.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to stop the inclusion of programs and
projects which are not among the programs and projects enumerated in Section 3.0
to 4.0 of the DILG-DBM Joint Circular. Instead of disbursing the 20% component
of the Internal Revenue Allotment for short-term programs and projects that would
benefit only a few constituents of the municipality, the developmental fund must be
directed to the attainment of desired social, economic development and
environmental outcomes that would benefit the municipality in the long-run.

We recommend that management stop using the 20% component of the
Internal Revenue Allotment which is intended for developmental projects for
entertainment like holding of Mr. & Miss Face Body Shot and Mr. & Miss Tolosa
2016, giving out prizes for games, talent and sports competitions, fireworks display,
rental of equipment and other various related expenses in the celebration of the
Tolosa Day and Summer Sports Festival since doing so is contrary to Section 343 of
the RA 7160 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October
29, 2012.

We recommend that the executive officers strictly adhere to the provisions
of DILG Memorandum Circular 2011-059 and COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid
the incurrence of unnecessary and excessive expenditures.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the personnel in charge of
granting the financial assistance to require the recipient individual or group to
submit (1) a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement embodying the terms and
conditions to be followed in the utilization assistance; (2) inspection report and
certificate of project completion rendered/issued by the GO authorized
representative upon completion of the project for the purpose of liquidation; and (3)
list of beneficiaries with their acceptance/acknowledgement of the
project/fund/goods/services received.
We also recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant-Designate
refrain from processing payments/releases of financial assistance if such are not
supported with the aforesaid supporting documents in order that all financial
assistance accrue to the benefit of the constituents.

We recommend that each accountable officer ensure the renewal of their
fidelity bond before its expiration date in compliance with Section 101 of PD 1445 and
Section 7.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009

We recommend that the Accounting Unit ensure that official receipts or
acknowledgement receipts are attached and signed by Payee on the received portion
of the DV consistent with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2004-006 dated September
9, 2004.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant
to stamp “PAID” all vouchers, payrolls and their supporting documents after the
corresponding check is signed and issued to the payees in strict compliance with
Section 2 (Q) of COA Circular No. 92-389 dated November 3, 1992.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer
to:
1. Refrain from drawing checks in the absence of certification from the
Municipal Accountant; and
2. Strictly adhere to paragraph 4 and 5 of Section 4 of the Government
Auditing Code of the Philippines (PD 1445), pertinent provisions of NGAS
Manual for LGUs and rules on internal control for disbursements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and Department Heads fast
track/ prioritize the implementation of prior years’ audit recommendations.

We recommend that management: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to demand from the Accountable Officers to remit their collections promptly and intact; b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to check first the inventory of accountable forms to justify the issuance of additional booklets. See to it that all issued accountable forms be submitted and likewise, collections thereof be remitted.
observations.reason: The MTO explained that field collectors could not immediately remit their collections daily because preparation of the report of collections and reconciliation with the actual collections on hand requires a lot of time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to strictly deposit all collections promptly and intact to ensure that government funds are properly accounted for and safeguarded against loss or misuse.
observations.reason: The Cash -Local Treasury Account has a monthly average balance of P 330,081.40 which shows that collections are not deposited promptly and intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer and all concerned officers to prepare and submit the monthly CRAAF and RAAF respectively, supported with requisition issue slips duly approved and properly filled up and the classification/type of accountable forms issued with serial numbers indicated.

We recommend that management: a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of the agency’s properties and submit the inventory report. b. Require the Accounting Unit and the General Services/Property Unit to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers, Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards respectively and reconcile the balances of the accounts in the general ledger against the individual subsidiary/property ledger cards including the inventory report prepared by the Property Unit.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016 the PPE account have a reported balance of P 67,570,402.77 or equivalent to 64.14% of the Total Assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the OIC-Accountant to use the proper classification to record accounting transactions charged accurately.
observations.reason: In the Accounting Unit some monetized leave credits are still recorded under the Terminal Leave Benefits account. The accounting staff admitted her difficulty in determining the appropriate debit/credit entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the CSC rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the project signboards and/or public notices to be posted at the project site, for validation.
observations.reason: Certified project notices / signboards are already posted at the project site, for validation consistent with regulations; financials should also be posted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provision of Section 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009 Rules on Settlement of Accounts.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor direct the Treasurer to see to it that all disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents of paid disbursement vouchers should be stamped PAID.
observations.reason: Although management asserted compliance, post-audit of DVs showed that not all disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents are stamped PAID. Reiterated in Finding No. 22, Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in preparing the Certified List of Taxpayers to serve as the basis of the Accountant in recording or setting-up the correct RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Require all collectors to remit their collection daily and intact to the treasury of the municipality. In case of failure to remit the collection on the same date, require the AO to submit, together with his Report of Collections and Remittance, and explanation for his failure to do so. b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor daily and full remittance of all the collections of Accountable Officers and to deposit daily and intact the remitted amount. c. Relieve from their duties those collectors who are habitually delayed in their remittance since such practice is an indication that funds are being misappropriated.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require these collectors to maintain cashbook in the prescribed format and update it daily as required in Section 26 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

We recommend that management: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer to conduct the physical count and maintain the supplies and inventory records; b. Require the Accounting Unit to record the receipt and issuance of Supplies and Inventory in the corresponding Supplies and Stock Cards; c. Require custodians of each department to prepare quarterly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) supported with Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and submit to the Accounting Unit; d. Require Property Custodian of each department to conduct a physical count of supplies on hand and prepare inventory list at year-end; e. Accounting Unit exhaust all possible means to produce the necessary inventory reports and records to support the non-moving accounts and file the appropriate request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is required to record the receipt and issuance of supplies in the books of accounts; however, reiterated in audit finding no. 12, Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to submit the following: (a) Composition of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council; (b) Municipal LDRRMP; (c) Annual Work and Financial Plan; (d) projects/activities undertaken and report on the status thereof; and (e) report on the utilization of the LDRRMF and other dedicated disaster risk reduction and management resources.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant provide and compute the correct allowance for depreciation and to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 4-0 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1 and Section 58 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: Depreciation provisions of P 9,535,064.12 or 27.43% of the total assets were provided to some PPEs; however no lapsing was provided for the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to implement the P4,000.00 net take home pay required for such employees for the current year provided for under the General Appropriations Act for CY 2013 also known as RA 10352.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: (1) Set up and maintain a special account in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund; (2) Strictly adhere to section 105 and 110 of the NGAS Manual Volume 1 for LGUs and prepare the post-closing Trial Balance for each special account, the Year-end Balance and financial statements; and (3) these reports should form part of the schedules of the General Fund Trial Balance.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel at the Accounting Unit who would prepare and maintain records for the Special Account in the GF for the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor direct the management concerned to stop the practice of procuring petroleum products thru reimbursement basis and that the standards set forth in the IRR of RA 9184 are observed. In addition discontinue the practice of procuring goods and services on reimbursement basis and strictly adhere to payment made by check pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated Feb. 10, 1997.
observations.reason: The BAC should see to it that the standards set forth in the IRR of RA9184 are observed. Management should discontinue the practice of procuring goods and services on reimbursement basis and strictly adhere to payment made by check pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to submit the LDRRMP, Annual Work and Financial Plan, projects/activities undertaken and report on status and utilization of LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Implemented
